faqs.question: How is a residential plot different from other land types on PGI?
faqs.answer: Residential plots are zoned specifically for housing construction, and each listing includes facing direction, road width, and utility access — details that matter most when you're planning to build a home.

faqs.question: What does the "RERA Registered" tag mean?
faqs.answer: It means the plot and its developer are registered under the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, giving you added legal protection and transparency on project timelines.

faqs.question: Can I schedule a physical site visit before buying?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every listing page lets you request a guided site visit with our local verification team, who can walk the boundaries with you in person.

faqs.question: Are the photos and measurements on each listing current?
faqs.answer: Yes — our verification team re-visits listings periodically, and each plot shows the date of its last on-site confirmation.

cta.title: Found your plot? Let's verify it together.
cta.description: Talk to our team about site visits, document checks, and everything else that goes into a residential purchase you won't regret.

faqs.question: How is agricultural land different from other land types on PGI?
faqs.answer: Agricultural land is zoned for farming use, and each listing includes soil type, irrigation source, and revenue record status — the details that matter most when you're planning to cultivate or invest for the long term.

faqs.question: Can non-farmers buy agricultural land in India?
faqs.answer: Rules vary by state — some states restrict agricultural land purchase to registered farmers. Our team shares the exact eligibility rules for each listing's state before you proceed.

faqs.question: How is soil quality verified?
faqs.answer: Our field team collects on-site samples and records soil type and fertility indicators, which are shared on the listing along with the date of testing.

faqs.question: How is industrial land different from other land types on PGI?
faqs.answer: Industrial land is zoned specifically for manufacturing or warehousing use, and each listing includes sanctioned power load, road width, and pollution clearance status — the details that matter most when setting up a facility.

faqs.question: What does "SPCB Approved" mean?
faqs.answer: It means the plot's industrial category has been cleared by the State Pollution Control Board for the type of operations it's zoned for.

faqs.question: Can I check the sanctioned power load before buying?

faqs.question: How is a mixed-use plot different from other land types on PGI?
faqs.answer: Mixed-use plots are zoned to allow both residential and commercial construction on the same parcel, and each listing includes the permissible FSI split for each use — the details that matter most for developers planning a combined project.

faqs.question: What does the "RERA Registered" tag mean?
faqs.answer: It means the plot and its developer are registered under the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, giving you added legal protection and transparency on project timelines.

faqs.question: Can I build only commercial or only residential on a mixed-use plot?
faqs.answer: Yes, in most cases — mixed-use zoning gives flexibility, but the exact permissible ratio varies by municipality. Our team shares the local zoning rules for each listing before you proceed.

faqs.answer: Yes — our verification team re-visits listings periodically, and each plot shows the date of its last on-site confirmation.

faqs.question: How is a plotted development different from a standalone residential plot?
faqs.answer: Plots inside a plotted development sit within a single approved layout with shared internal roads, drainage, and often a clubhouse or security — and each listing includes the layout's official sanction number.

faqs.question: What does the "RERA Registered" tag mean?
faqs.answer: It means the plot and its developer are registered under the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, giving you added legal protection and transparency on project timelines.

faqs.question: Is the layout approval checked for the entire development, or just my plot?
faqs.answer: We verify the layout's overall sanction from the development authority first, then confirm your specific plot's dimensions and title against that approved plan.

faqs.question: Are the photos and measurements on each listing current?
faqs.answer: Yes — our verification team re-visits listings periodically, and each plot shows the date of its last on-site confirmation.

faqs.question: How is farm land different from agricultural land on PGI?
faqs.answer: Farm land listings are curated for buyers who also want a weekend home or farmhouse on the parcel, so each listing includes farmhouse construction eligibility alongside soil and irrigation details.

faqs.question: Can I legally build a farmhouse on farm land?
faqs.answer: It depends on the state and district norms — some areas permit a limited built-up area for farmhouse use. Our team confirms the exact eligibility for each listing before you proceed.

faqs.question: Is the water source tested for both drinking and irrigation use?
faqs.answer: Our field team records the water source type and depth on-site; for drinking-water suitability we recommend an independent lab test, which we can help arrange.

faqs.question: Are the photos and measurements on each listing current?
faqs.answer: Yes — our verification team re-visits listings periodically, and each parcel shows the date of its last on-site confirmation.

faqs.question: How is a hill view plot different from other land types on PGI?
faqs.answer: Hill view plots are assessed for slope stability and view orientation in addition to the standard checks, and each listing confirms the exact sightline — valley, ridge, or forest — verified in person.

faqs.question: Is it safe to build on a sloped hill plot?
faqs.answer: Slope gradient and soil-retention capacity are assessed for every listed plot; steep or unstable sections are flagged clearly so you know what retaining or foundation work may be needed.

faqs.question: Is the road to the plot accessible year-round?
faqs.answer: We verify all-weather accessibility as part of the site visit, and any seasonal access limitations — such as monsoon landslide risk — are noted on the listing.

faqs.question: Are the photos and measurements on each listing current?
faqs.answer: Yes — our verification team re-visits listings periodically, and each plot shows the date of its last on-site confirmation.
